hints.title: Nudge
hints.content: The registration form is safe. But what happens after your data is stored? Is it always handled safely when it's used later?

hints.title: Direction
hints.content: The password reset reads your username from the database and uses it in an UPDATE query without parameterization. If your username contains SQL, it gets executed during password reset.

hints.title: Approach
hints.content: Register with a username that will break out of the UPDATE query. The reset query is: UPDATE users SET password='...' WHERE username='<your_stored_username>'. Craft a username that closes the WHERE clause and adds another statement or modifies the query.

hints.title: Near-solution
hints.content: Register with username: admin'-- This makes the reset UPDATE set the password for 'admin' (since everything after -- is commented out). Then log in as admin to see the flag on the dashboard.

hints.title: Solution
hints.content: 1) Register with username: admin'-- and any password/email. 2) Log in with your new account (username: admin'--, password: your_password). 3) Go to password reset page and change password to something like 'hacked'. 4) The UPDATE becomes: UPDATE users SET password='hacked' WHERE username='admin'--'. This resets admin's password. 5) Log out and log in as admin/hacked. The admin dashboard displays the flag.
